claim_boundary: stress-slice synthesis from tracked compact artifacts; not benchmark-strength, paper-facing, or hardware-calibrated AMV ranking evidence

source_artifacts.role: broadest existing paired AMMV/default multi-scenario, multi-seed slice
per-scenario and per-seed paired comparison table
single-scenario predecessor showing frame-identical AMMV/default rows
one-scenario, one-seed actuation-aware feasibility ordering predecessor
diagnostic-only actuation feasibility ranking synthesis that triggered the generalization audit

decision.reason: Existing multi-scenario paired AMMV/default rows expose no mechanism or outcome differences, while the actuation-aware feasibility ordering remains limited to one scenario and one seed.
decision.stop_rule: stop as diagnostic-only; do not claim a general AMV feasibility ranking until direct actuation-aware paired rows exist across scenario families and seeds

disagreement_cases.summary: The only nonzero feasibility-ordering signal is a one-scenario, one-seed actuation-aware slice; the broader multi-scenario AMMV/default slice is frame-identical and cannot rank mechanisms.
Tracked compact AMMV/default rows include frame and force-vector deltas, but all are zero; they do not expose a discriminating actuation-aware mechanism signal.

validation: Parsed tracked issue_2434 summary.json for row counts, scenario families, seeds, and zero-delta maxima.
Inspected tracked issue_2434 candidate_pair_comparison.csv for required scenario, seed, status, metric, and mechanism-delta fields.
Inspected tracked issue_2224 comparison.json and docs/context/issue_2446_amv_feasibility_ranking.md for the one-scenario actuation-aware predecessor boundary.
